# AIRelays

AIRelays is a local OpenAI-compatible HTTP server with provider-scoped runtimes. The default runtime uses an AIRelays-owned ChatGPT subscription login. An optional experimental Claude runtime uses the local `claude` CLI for explicit `claude:*` text models. AIRelays protects `/v1/*` and `/no-tools/v1/*` with its own relay bearer token by default and can run in open local relay mode only when the Claude runtime is disabled.

## Key Docs

- [README.md](README.md)
- [docs/getting-started.md](docs/getting-started.md)
- [docs/configuration.md](docs/configuration.md)
- [docs/security.md](docs/security.md)
- [docs/api.md](docs/api.md)
- [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md)
- [docs/subscription-status.md](docs/subscription-status.md)
- [docs/faq.md](docs/faq.md)
- [docs/troubleshooting.md](docs/troubleshooting.md)
- [DISCLAIMER.md](DISCLAIMER.md)

## Main Commands

- `airelays init`
- `airelays login`
- `airelays serve`
- `airelays init --no-auth`
- `airelays serve --no-auth`
- `claude auth login --claudeai`
- `claude setup-token`
- `airelays status`
- `airelays token show`
- `airelays token rotate`

## Important Notes

- OpenAI runtime: first-class `responses`, `chat.completions`, and `completions`
- Claude runtime: experimental text `chat.completions` and text `completions` only
- models starting with `claude:` route to the Claude runtime when it is enabled
- other model ids route to the OpenAI runtime when it is enabled
- Claude runtime requires bearer auth and loopback binding
- `GET /v1/subscription/status` applies to the OpenAI runtime only
